enthusiast.text: Minimal enthusiast following — it's a family appliance with a stellar durability reputation. The community (ToyotaNation, r/ToyotaHighlander) focuses on towing setups, road-trip builds, and high-mileage maintenance bragging rights; 300,000-mile 2GR examples are common. XSE has mild curb-appeal following.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Honda Pilot, Kia Telluride, Hyundai Palisade, Mazda CX-90, Ford Explorer, Nissan Pathfinder. The Telluride/Palisade and CX-90 feel richer inside; the Highlander counters with the best hybrid mpg in class (36 combined), top reliability scores (Consumer Reports/J.D. Power), and stronger resale — the safe choice rather than the exciting one. Buyers needing more third-row space are directed to the Grand Highlander (2024+).

volkswagen-jetta.subtitle: 4-door compact sedan; Mk6/Mk6.5 (A6, Typ 1B, PQ35) 2015–2018; Mk7 (A7, MQB A1) 2019–2026 (facelifts 2022 and 2025)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: Mk6 2015: 2.0L MPI EA113 8v I4 (115 hp, base S), 1.8T EA888 Gen3 1,798cc turbo I4 (170 hp), 2.0 TDI EA288 1,968cc turbo-diesel I4 (150 hp, 2015–2016 only — Dieselgate stop-sale), 1.4T EA211 Hybrid (170 hp combined, 2015–2016), GLI 2.0T EA888 Gen3 1,984cc (210 hp). Mk6.5 2016–2018: 1.4T EA211 1,395cc turbo I4 (150 hp) replaces 2.0L; 1.8T EA888 (170 hp) through 2017; GLI 2.0T (210 hp). Mk7 2019–2021: 1.4T EA211 (147 hp) only; GLI 2.0T EA888 Gen3 (228 hp). Mk7 facelift 2022–2026: 1.5T EA211 evo 1,498cc Miller-cycle VTG turbo I4 (158 hp); GLI 2.0T EA888 (228 hp).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: Mk6: 5-speed manual, 6-speed Aisin torque-converter automatic (09G Tiptronic) on 1.4T/1.8T, 6-speed DSG DQ250 on TDI/GLI, 7-speed DSG DQ200 on Hybrid. Mk7: 6-speed manual (S/R-Line/Sport, GLI), 8-speed Aisin AWF8F35 torque-converter automatic; GLI: 6MT or 7-speed DSG DQ381. Manual dropped from non-GLI Jetta for 2025 refresh; GLI manual ends after 2026 MY (DSG-only 2027).

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: FWD only, all generations and trims (no AWD Jetta offered in the US 2015–2026).

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: Mk6: L 182.2–182.8 in / W 70.0 in / H 57.2–58.3 in / WB 104.3 in / curb 2,870–3,208 lb. Mk7: L 185.1 in / W 70.8 in / H 57.4 in / WB 105.7 in / curb 2,888–2,970 lb (base powertrain; GLI ≈3,300 lb — Not verified, needs OEM source).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Mk6: J.D. Power 2015 Jetta 79/100 overall, Quality & Reliability 78/100 ("Average"); IIHS 2015 Top Safety Pick+; the simple 2.0L MPI/6AT is the durable low-risk powertrain. Mk7: J.D. Power 2020 Jetta consumer score 86/100; 742 NHTSA owner complaints logged for 2019–2025 (top areas: electrical system, engine, transmission); IIHS 2022 crashworthiness all "Good," headlights Marginal/Poor by trim.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: Mk6: EA888 Gen3 1.8T water pump/thermostat housing assembly leaks (common fail point from ~4 years/60k; OE part family 06L121111/06L121012); TDI models subject to Dieselgate stop-sale/buyback (EA288); panoramic/sunroof drain maintenance per factory schedule. Mk7: in-tank low-pressure fuel pump failures and recall coverage on 2019–2024 cars (stall risk; shares part family with Taos); NHTSA complaint clusters in electrical, engine, and transmission categories; early MIB infotainment glitches. The 8-speed Aisin automatic has proven robust.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: VW factory schedule (US): oil/filter every 10,000 mi/12 mo alternating minor/standard services (VW 502 00 5W-40 on 1.4T Mk6.5; 508 00 0W-20 on later EA211 evo/EA888); DSG fluid + filter every 40,000 mi (DQ250/DQ381/DQ200); brake fluid first change at 3 years then every 2 years; spark plugs every 40,000 mi on 2.0T (40k–60k on 1.4T/1.5T — confirm against model-year booklet); coolant is long-life G12evo/G13 — check level/freeze protection at each standard service, no fixed US replacement interval; dust/pollen filter every 40,000 mi/2 yr; sunroof drains checked at services. Timing: EA888/EA113 chain-driven (no scheduled replacement); EA211 1.4T/1.5T belt-driven — inspect per schedule, no fixed US interval listed.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: Mk6 1.8T ≈7.9 s (C&D); Mk7 1.5T 7.6 s (C&D, 2022 SEL); Mk7 GLI 6.1 s (C&D, 2022 Autobahn 6MT); Mk6 GLI ≈6.1–6.4 s. Performance trims: GLI (all years; Mk7 GLI adds multi-link rear axle and VAQ electronically controlled limited-slip diff), Jetta Sport 1.8T (2015–2017, sport suspension), GLI Edition 30 (2014), GLI 35th Anniversary (2019), GLI 40th Anniversary Edition (2024).

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: GLI is the tuner focus: APR/Unitronic/Cobb Stage 1–2 ECU tunes on the EA888 Gen3 (large gains, IS20/IS38 turbo swaps), downpipes, intercoolers, cat-back exhausts; lowering springs/coilovers and sway bars from the VW aftermarket (ShopDAP, UroTuning catalogs); 1.4T/1.5T EA211 tunes exist but gains are modest. Factory performance variants: GLI Autobahn (DCC adaptive dampers on Mk7), anniversary editions above.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The Mk6 (NCS) was VW's cost-optimized North American sedan on a PQ35 derivative with a twist-beam rear axle on lower trims (multi-link restored across the US range for the 2015 facelift; GLI always multi-link). The Mk7 moved to MQB A1 with a longer wheelbase and body, standard LED lighting, and available Digital Cockpit, but reverted non-GLI trims to a torsion-beam rear axle; GLI keeps multi-link + VAQ e-LSD. The 2022 facelift brought the EA211 evo 1.5T (Miller cycle, variable-geometry turbo) replacing the long-serving 1.4T.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The GLI is viewed as "a GTI with a trunk" and has deep aftermarket support; it was also the last manual-transmission VW sold in North America. Base Jettas are seen as value commuters — the Mk6 drew criticism for decontented interiors, while the Mk7 is respected for refinement-per-dollar but ignored by most enthusiasts outside the GLI.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Honda Civic, Toyota Corolla, Mazda3, Hyundai Elantra. The Jetta counters with low-end turbo torque, a roomy back seat/trunk, and autobahn-flavored ride tuning; long-term reliability trails Corolla/Civic, but the GLI undercuts the Civic Si and GTI on price while matching their pace.

volkswagen-passat.subtitle: 4-door midsize sedan; NMS Gen 1 (A32/A33, PQ46, Chattanooga) 2015–2019 (2016 facelift); NMS Gen 2 (2020 reskin, still PQ46) 2020–2022 — US Passat discontinued after 2022 MY (Limited Edition, 1,973 units)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2015–2017: 1.8T EA888 Gen3 1,798cc turbo I4 (170 hp); 2015–2016: 2.0 TDI EA288 1,968cc turbo-diesel I4 (150 hp — Dieselgate stop-sale); 2012–2018: 3.6 VR6 FSI EA390 3,597cc narrow-angle V6 (280 hp); 2018–2019: 2.0T EA888 Gen3 1,984cc (174 hp) replaces 1.8T; 2020–2022: 2.0T EA888 Gen 3B "B-cycle" (174 hp) only engine. (2.5L EA855 I5 ended 2013 — pre-scope.)

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 1.8T: 5-speed manual (2014–2015) or 6-speed Aisin torque-converter automatic (09G); 2.0T (2018–2022): 6-speed Aisin automatic only; VR6: 6-speed DSG DQ250; TDI: 6-speed manual or 6-speed DSG DQ250. No 8-speed or DSG on the 2020–2022 car.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: 1.8T: 184 lb-ft. 2.0T: 184 lb-ft (2018–19, Wikipedia spec table); 2020–22 advertised at 206 lb-ft — Not verified against retrieved OEM source, confirm on VW media site. TDI: 236 lb-ft @ 1,750. VR6: 258 lb-ft.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: FWD only, all years/trims (no AWD US Passat NMS).

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: Gen 1: L 191.7 in / W 72.2 in / H 58.0 in / WB 110.4 in / curb ≈3,260 lb (1.8T, 1,480 kg) to 3,571 lb (VR6, 1,620 kg) per Carfolio conversions. Gen 2: L 191.9 in / W 72.2 in / H 58.5 in / WB 110.4 in / curb 3,276–3,369 lb (1,486–1,528 kg, Carfolio).

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: 2016–2017 1.8T 6AT: 25/38/29 (highway 38 per EPA figure cited in NMS documentation). 2018 2.0T: 25/36/29. 2018 VR6: 19/28/22. 2020 2.0T: 23/34/27 (fueleconomy.gov). TDI (2015): EA288 ~31/42/35 manual — Not verified this session, needs OEM source.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: The NMS is regarded as one of VW's more trouble-free US products of the era — simple, proven powertrains and a conventional automatic; Motor Trend 2012 Car of the Year; Car and Driver midsize comparison winner (March 2012). Weak points tracked below. NHTSA recall: 2016–2019 front seat-back adjustment levers may not engage (injury risk in crash). Takata airbag recalls touch most model years (check VIN at nhtsa.gov/recalls).

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: EA888 Gen3 water pump/thermostat housing assembly leaks (common fail point, as early as 4 years; 06L121111/06L121012 part family) on 1.8T/2.0T; intake-valve carbon buildup on direct-injection EA888 at higher mileage (owner-documented); TDI models caught in Dieselgate (buyback/fix; verify emissions campaign completion before service upsell); 2016–2019 seat-recliner lever recall; the discontinued 2.5L I5 (pre-2015) was the durable fleet favorite.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: VW factory schedule (US): oil/filter every 10,000 mi/12 mo (VW 502 00 spec); DSG fluid + filter every 40,000 mi (VR6/TDI DQ250); brake fluid first at 3 years then every 2 years; spark plugs every 40,000 mi (2.0T; 1.8T per booklet 40k–60k — confirm by model year); long-life coolant, level/freeze check each standard service; Passat-specific: sunroof water drains checked/cleaned at every service per factory schedule. Timing: all NMS engines chain-driven (no scheduled replacement). TDI additionally: DEF/AdBlue service (SCR system on EA288).

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: 1.8T ≈7.7–7.9 s; 2020–2022 2.0T ≈7.8–8.0 s; VR6 ≈6.4 s (period MotorTrend test — Not re-verified this session, needs OEM source). Performance-adjacent trims: 2018 Passat GT (VR6, sport suspension, 19-in wheels, black roof), R-Line appearance trims.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Thin enthusiast scene. ECU tunes exist for the 2020–2024 2.0T EA888 Gen 3B (e.g., Boost Dynamic Tuning Stage 1–2, remote OBD flash — VW detuned this engine to 174 hp, leaving headroom); VR6 cars get intake/exhaust work; otherwise mostly maintenance-oriented ownership.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The NMS was purpose-built for North America on a stretched PQ46 platform in Chattanooga with 80% US-sourced content — 3.9 in longer than the European B7 with a huge rear seat. The 2016 facelift added MIB II infotainment, LED lighting options, and driver assists. Notably, the 2020 "new" Passat was a reskin on the same 2011-vintage PQ46 architecture rather than MQB — an explicit cost decision late in the nameplate's life.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Minimal enthusiast following; the VR6 and 2018 GT have a small niche as sleepers. The community values NMS Passats as cheap, roomy highway cars, and Dieselgate-era TDIs have a fuel-miser cult (2013 TDI held a Guinness 48-state economy record).

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Toyota Camry, Honda Accord, Nissan Altima, Ford Fusion, Hyundai Sonata. The Passat wins on rear-seat space and ride comfort; it loses on resale and long-term reliability perception versus Camry/Accord, which is why clean examples are bargain used buys — verify water pump and carbon-cleaning history.

volkswagen-tiguan.subtitle: 5-door compact SUV; Mk1 (5N, PQ46) 2015–2018 (sold 2017–2018 as Tiguan Limited alongside Mk2); Mk2 (AD/BW, MQB A2, LWB-only US) 2018–2024 (2022 facelift); Mk3 (CT1, MQB Evo) 2025–2026

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: Mk1 (2015–2018 incl. Limited): 2.0 TSI EA888 Gen 2 (CCTA/CBFA) 1,984cc turbo I4. Mk2 (2018–2024): 2.0 TSI EA888 Gen 3B "B-cycle" (Budack) 1,984cc turbo I4. Mk3 (2025–2026): 2.0 TSI EA888 evo4 1,984cc turbo I4. Single engine offered per generation in the US.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: Mk1: 6-speed Aisin torque-converter automatic (09M). Mk2: 8-speed Aisin torque-converter automatic (only US gearbox — no DSG). Mk3: 8-speed automatic. All with FWD standard; no manual offered in the US.

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: Mk1: 200 hp (US rating; 200 PS/197 hp metric). Mk2: 184 hp. Mk3: 201 hp @ 5,000.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: FWD standard, 4Motion AWD (Haldex coupling) optional on all generations. Mk2: third-row seat standard on FWD, optional on AWD until it was dropped for the 2022 facelift; Mk3 is two-row/5-seat only in the US.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: Mk1: L 174.3–174.5 in / W 71.2 in / H 65.6–66.4 in / WB 102.5 in / curb weight — Not verified, needs OEM source (≈3,400–3,800 lb est.). Mk2 (US LWB): L 185.5–185.8 in / W 72.4–73.2 in / H 65.9 in / WB 109.9 in / curb 3,825 lb (AWD, 1,735 kg); FWD lighter — exact figure needs OEM source. Mk3: L 184.4 in / W 73.0 in / H 66.5 in / WB 109.9 in / curb 3,801 lb (AWD base, J.D. Power); ~170 lb lighter than Mk2 per VW (aluminum suspension content).

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: Mk1/Tiguan Limited 2018 (same powertrain): 20/26/23 (FWD), 19/25/22 (AWD), premium fuel. Mk2 2018: 22/27/24 (FWD), 21/27/23 (AWD). Mk2 2024 SEL R-Line 4Motion: 22/29 EPA C/H label (C&D observed 26). Mk3 2025: 26/34/29 (FWD S, fueleconomy.gov), 22/30 (AWD; combined 25–26).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Mk1: weakest of the family — Consumer Reports reliability below average for the era; NHTSA 2016 AWD 5-star overall; IIHS small-overlap "Marginal." Mk2: improved but still mid-pack; Mexican-built LWB scored 5 stars Latin NCAP (2019) with AEB/pedestrian awards; MIB2 electronics complaints common. Mk3: too new for meaningful data; IIHS 2025 testing returned a "Poor" updated moderate-overlap front rating (other crashworthiness tests Good) — noteworthy for a family SUV.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: Mk1: timing chain tensioner failure on the 2.0T CCTA/CBFA — documented in VW TSB (2012) and a US class-action settlement (timingchain-settlement.com) covering 2008–2013 vehicles; revised tensioner fitted from ~2014, but chain/tensioner inspection remains mandatory diligence on any Mk1; water pump failures also documented. Mk2: water pump/thermostat housing leaks (EA888 Gen 3 family part issue); MIB2 touchscreen delamination/digitizer failure on 2018–2021 cars (ghost touch/dead touch — aftermarket digitizer fixes exist, dealer replaces head unit); panoramic sunroof drains need periodic cleaning per schedule. Mk3: none documented yet (new model).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: VW factory schedule (US): oil/filter every 10,000 mi/12 mo (502 00; 508 00 0W-20 on Mk3); brake fluid first at 3 years then every 2 years; spark plugs ~40,000 mi on 2.0T (confirm per model year); long-life coolant with level/freeze checks; DSG not fitted to US cars — no DSG service needed; 4Motion models: Haldex coupling oil service every 3 years (confirm against model-year booklet); transfer/rear-final-drive checks at standard services; sunroof drains checked at services. Timing: all engines chain-driven (Mk1 tensioner is a wear item, see Known issues).

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: Mk1 — Not verified, needs OEM source (period tests ≈8 s); Mk2 8.2 s (C&D, 2024 SEL R-Line 4Motion); Mk3 7.7 s (C&D, 2025 SE). Factory performance variant: Tiguan R (320 PS/316 hp, torque-vectoring AWD, 21-in wheels, Akrapovič option) — Europe only, never sold in the US; US gets R-Line appearance/trim packages only. Mk3 tows 1,800 lb (AWD).

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Light but real scene: Stage 1 ECU tunes on the Mk2 EA888 Gen 3B (APR/Unitronic) unlock meaningful torque; Mk1 2.0T responds to the mature EA888 Gen 2 tuning catalog; typical bolt-ons are intake, intercooler, cat-back, lowering springs, and wheels from the VW aftermarket (ShopDAP/UroTuning). Mk3 support is emerging.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The Mk1 sat on PQ46 (Passat B6-related — commonly misreported as PQ35) and was imported from Germany. The Mk2 moved to MQB A2 and, in the US, is sold only as the Mexico-built long-wheelbase body with a conventional 8-speed automatic replacing the DSGs used elsewhere — a deliberate durability/cost choice. The Mk3 adopts MQB Evo with more aluminum in the suspension and high-strength hot-formed steel, cutting ~170 lb, and moves to MIB4 electronics with a 12.9-in screen.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Not a cult vehicle — the community sees it as the practical family VW with GTI-adjacent drivetrain DNA; the forbidden-fruit Tiguan R is the enthusiast talking point. Mk1 Limiteds are cheap used entries with known tensioner baggage; Mk2s are valued for space and refinement rather than speed.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Toyota RAV4, Honda CR-V, Mazda CX-5, Hyundai Tucson, Subaru Forester. The Tiguan counters with the best ride/refinement balance and standard turbo torque; it trails RAV4/CR-V on reliability record and (pre-2025) on fuel economy — the Mk3's 26/34 FWD rating largely closes the MPG gap.

volkswagen-golf.subtitle: 5-door hatchback; Mk7/Mk7.5 (MQB) 2015–2021 (incl. GTI, Golf R, SportWagen/Alltrack, e-Golf, 2015 TDI); Mk8/Mk8.5 (MQB Evo) 2022–2026 — US Mk8 range is GTI and Golf R only (facelift/Mk8.5 from 2025 MY)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: Mk7 Golf (2015–2017): 1.8T EA888 Gen3 1,798cc turbo I4 (170 hp); 2015 only: 2.0 TDI EA288 (150 hp — Dieselgate stop-sale); e-Golf BEV (115 hp/136 hp). Mk7.5 Golf (2018): 1.8T carried over; (2019–2021): 1.4T EA211 1,395cc (147 hp) replaces 1.8T. GTI (all Mk7/Mk7.5): 2.0T EA888 Gen3 1,984cc (210 hp 2015–2017; 220 hp 2018; 228 hp 2019–2021). Golf R Mk7/7.5: 2.0T EA888 Gen3 (292 hp US, 2015–2019). Mk8 GTI (2022–2024): 2.0T EA888 evo4 (241 hp). Mk8 Golf R (2022–2024): 2.0T EA888 evo4 (315 hp). Mk8.5 (2025–2026): GTI 261 hp; Golf R 328 hp. SportWagen/Alltrack (2015–2019): 1.8T 170 hp, FWD or 4Motion.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: Golf 1.8T: 5MT or 6-speed Aisin auto; Golf 1.4T (2019+): 6MT or 8-speed Aisin auto. GTI: 6MT or 6-speed DSG DQ250 (2015–2018), 7-speed DSG DQ381 (2019+); Mk8 GTI 6MT/7DSG — manual discontinued after 2024 (2024 GTI 380 special edition marked the MT farewell; Mk8.5 GTI is DSG-only). Golf R: 6MT or 6DSG (Mk7)/7DSG (Mk7.5+); Mk8 R 6MT (NA-only) or 7DSG — manual dropped for Mk8.5. SportWagen/Alltrack 4Motion: 6MT or 6-speed DSG DQ250.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: Golf/GTI: FWD only. Golf R: 4Motion AWD — Haldex 5th-gen (Mk7), updated system with torque-vectoring rear differential and Drift Mode (Mk8). SportWagen: FWD standard, 4Motion optional from 2017; Alltrack: 4Motion standard.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: Mk7 hatch: L 167.5–168.5 in / W 70.5–70.8 in / H 56.5–59.6 in / WB 103.6–103.8 in (103.1–103.5 facelift) / curb 2,657–3,307 lb (family incl. wagons/AWD). Mk8 hatch: L 168.7 in / W 70.4 in / H 57.3 in / WB 103.8 in / curb 2,767–3,230 lb.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Mk7 is one of the stronger VW reliability stories of the decade — 2015 North American Car of the Year and Motor Trend Car of the Year; IIHS 2021 Golf all-"Good" crashworthiness (headlights Poor); CR rated Mk7 Golf/GTI average to above average. Mk8: strong crash/safety showing — 2022 GTI IIHS Top Safety Pick, 2022 Golf R Top Safety Pick+ — but dragged by electronics: the MIB3 infotainment and haptic controls are widely criticized ("frustrating infotainment system" — U.S. News; "a step backwards" — Which?). JD Power/CR place Mk8 at about average, with complaints concentrated in software, not powertrain.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: Mk7/7.5: EA888 Gen3 water pump/thermostat housing leaks (1.8T, GTI, R — the generation's signature repair, OE 06L121111/06L121012 family); intake-valve carbon buildup on DI engines at mileage; sunroof drain clogs causing water ingress (documented on GTI/Golf/R); 2015 TDI Dieselgate (stop-sale/buyback/fix); DQ250 DSG mechatronic wear at high mileage. Mk8: MIB3 software glitches (lag, blank screens, CarPlay dropouts), touch-slider/haptic control frustration (Mk8.5 restores physical steering-wheel buttons), early build software recalls/updates; powertrain (EA888 evo4) has been largely trouble-free to date.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: VW factory schedule (US): oil/filter every 10,000 mi/12 mo (502 00 on Mk7; 508 00 0W-20 on Mk8); DSG fluid + filter every 40,000 mi (DQ250/DQ381 — GTI/R/SportWagen/Alltrack); brake fluid first at 3 years then every 2 years; spark plugs every 40,000 mi on 2.0T (GTI/R), 1.8T/1.4T per booklet (40k–60k — confirm by model year); long-life coolant, level/freeze checks at services; AWD models: Haldex coupling oil every 3 years. Timing: EA888 chain-driven; EA211 1.4T belt-driven (inspect per schedule, no fixed US interval). e-Golf: EV-specific schedule, no engine service.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: GTI Mk7 5.9–6.3 s (official 0–100 km/h 6.3–6.5); Mk8 GTI 5.2–5.9 s across instrumented tests (C&D/MT data points: 5.2 best, 5.6–5.9 typical); Golf R Mk7 4.9 s (official, DSG); Mk8 R 4.7 s official (0–100 km/h) with magazine tests in the 3.9–4.6 s range. Performance variants: GTI Performance Package (VAQ LSD, bigger brakes — standard from 2019 in the US), GTI Rabbit Edition (2019), GTI 380 (2024, MT send-off), Golf R 20 Years (2022)/R 333 (2023, not US); Mk7 GTI Clubsport/Clubsport S/TCR not sold in the US.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: The deepest tuner ecosystem in the segment: Cobb Accessport (Mk8 support), APR/Unitronic Stage 1–3 ECU/TCU tunes, IS38 turbo swaps on GTI, downpipes, intercoolers, cat-backs; coilovers (e.g., Godspeed MonoRS Mk8), sway bars, camber plates; DSG tunes to hold added torque. Golf R Stage 2 cars commonly exceed 400 hp. e-Golf and 1.4T Golf see light mods (wheels, springs).

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: Mk7 was the first MQB-platform Golf — lighter and roomier than Mk6, with the EA888 Gen3 family across GTI/R; the 2018 facelift (Mk7.5) added digital cockpit options and bumped GTI output. Mk8 moved to MQB Evo (Cd 0.275 vs 0.29), fully digital cockpit, shift-by-wire DSG, Travel Assist, and Car2X networking, plus a Vehicle Dynamics Manager integrating VAQ diff and DCC dampers; the 2025 Mk8.5 facelift added MIB4, more power (GTI 261 hp, R 328 hp), and restored physical steering-wheel buttons after customer backlash.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The GTI is the benchmark hot hatch and the center of the VW scene in North America — plaid seats, annual shows, and unmatched parts support; the Golf R is the cult all-weather weapon (473 Spektrum custom-color Mk7 Rs built for NA). The Mk8's software stung its reputation early, but the Mk8.5 power bump and button restoration have rehabilitated it; losing the manual after 2024 is the community's big grievance.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Honda Civic Si/Type R, Hyundai Elantra N, Subaru WRX, Mazda3, Toyota GR Corolla (R territory). GTI remains the refinement/balance pick — quieter and more adult than Elantra N, sharper than WRX; Golf R trades the Type R's edge for year-round usability and stealth. Reliability is mid-pack — better than older VWs' reputation, behind the Hondas/Toyotas, with electronics, not engines, the main complaint on Mk8.

mappings.note: Model name 'Optima / K5' is historically inaccurate for the K5; Optima was a distinct predecessor model. K5 replaced Optima in 2020. Suggest 'K5' only.
GTI is a trim/variant of the Golf, not a separate model ID in most OEM catalogs. 'Golf' covers both.
